prompt:
Assign GRADE certainty ratings to each evidence item below.
Ratings: HIGH (systematic review of RCTs, low heterogeneity), MODERATE (RCT with some concerns, or consistent observational), LOW (observational studies, high heterogeneity, indirect evidence), VERY_LOW (case reports, expert opinion, serious limitations).

For each item, output ONLY the number and rating, one per line:
1. HIGH
2. MODERATE
...

EVIDENCE:
1. [SILVER] Source: Effect of Thermal Aging on the Tensile Bond Strength at Redu | Statement: The resistance of resin-dentin bonds to thermal-aging degradation was material dependent
2. [GOLD] Source: Aging Methods-An Evaluation of Their Influence on Bond Stren | Statement: Three-way ANOVA showed substrate (p=0.00) and aging method (p=0.00) significantly affected bond strength but adhesive approach did not (p=0.84)
3. [SILVER] Source: Aging Methods-An Evaluation of Their Influence on Bond Stren | Statement: Adhesive/mixed failures were predominant for all groups regardless of aging method
4. [SILVER] Source: Encapsulating perovskite solar cells to withstand damp heat  | Statement: The IEC 61646 damp heat test requires 1000 hours at 85 degrees C and 85% relative humidity to assess resilience to heat and moisture
5. [SILVER] Source: Encapsulating perovskite solar cells to withstand damp heat  | Statement: EVA encapsulant decomposes into acetic acid under damp heat which reacts with perovskite causing yellow PbI2 degradation
